brief_justification: Correctly identifies 19:00 UTC and meeting appearing at 4 PM local, matching the ground truth exactly; goes three levels deep — identifies the bug, traces root cause to naive offset arithmetic, and prescribes fixes with specific library references; proceeds linearly without naming assumptions or checking its own reasoning method; cross-verifies by computing both the stored (wrong) and correct UTC values side by side; presents all conclusions as certain with no acknowledgment of edge cases (e.g., what if booking was made before DST began) or stated confidence levels; mentions two fix alternatives (IANA name storage vs. correct UTC resolution at booking) but does not evaluate tradeoffs between them; every conclusion is explicitly derived with offset arithmetic shown step by step, reproducible by a third party.
